ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Kit Kat as a branded product. Closest parent would be 'chocolate wafer confection' but no exact URI is available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chocolate wafer bar (closest parent match — FoodEx2 A0HCB)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0HCB is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a chocolate-enrobed wafer bar. No exact branded product code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Kit Kat 2-finger bar (21g) as sold individually at NTUC FairPrice, 7-Eleven, and Singapore convenience stores.

nutrient_source.title: Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar — Nestlé Singapore Product Label (2024); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central — Chocolate-covered wafer bar (FDC ID 169655)
nutrient_source.organization: Nestlé Singapore Pte Ltd; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary per-100g values sourced from Nestlé Singapore product label (2024), which is an approved manufacturer label source for packaged products. Micronutrient values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central chocolate wafer bar entry for plausibility. Values are consistent with USDA reference data for this product category.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Chocolate Wafer Bar (Kit Kat analogous) — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~55 for milk chocolate wafer bar is consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database values for chocolate-enrobed wafer biscuits. Fat content from cocoa butter and palm oil in the wafer cream moderates glucose absorption, placing GI below plain biscuits (~70). GL per 21g bar = (55 × 13g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 7.15, rounded to 6.9 per Nestlé label carbohydrate figure. Confidence: 0.9 (referenced analogous estimate).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content is negligible at ~20mg per 21g bar (95mg/100g); saturated fat 3.5g/bar is a secondary consideration in metabolic syndrome context
hypertension.guidance: Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar poses minimal sodium risk for hypertension at standard serving (20mg/bar); however, the saturated fat content (3.5g/bar, 16.7g/100g) warrants moderation in patients with co-occurring metabolic syndrome or dyslipidaemia, where saturated fat contributes to cardiovascular risk compounding hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 9g added sugar and GL ~7 per 21g bar; not a free food — must be accounted for in carbohydrate budget
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar has GI ~55 (low) and GL ~7 per bar, which is moderate; it is not a free food for Type 2 Diabetes management — the 9g sugar and 13g total carbohydrate per bar must be counted within the daily carbohydrate budget, and consumption should be limited to 1–2 bars per week as an occasional treat rather than a daily snack.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 16.7g/100g (3.5g/bar) from palm oil and cocoa butter; high sugar 42.9g/100g contributes to atherogenic dietary pattern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar contains 3.5g saturated fat per bar (primarily from palm oil in wafer cream and cocoa butter), contributing meaningfully to saturated fat intake in the context of cardiovascular disease dietary management; daily consumption would add ~24.5g saturated fat/week from this source alone, which is clinically significant and should be limited to occasional consumption in patients with established CVD or high cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat from palm oil and cocoa butter, and high sugar content, contribute to pro-inflammatory dietary pattern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For rheumatoid arthritis, the saturated fat (3.5g/bar from palm oil and cocoa butter) and sugar (9g/bar) in Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar add to a pro-inflammatory dietary pattern; daily consumption is not recommended, and occasional intake (1–2×/week) should be considered within the context of an overall anti-inflammatory diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kit Kat 2-Finger Bar (Singapore, 21g) is a factory-manufactured ultra-processed chocolate wafer confection produced and distributed by Nestlé Singapore Pte Ltd, sold individually at NTUC FairPrice, 7-Eleven, and convenience stores across Singapore. Each 21g bar consists of two crisp wafer fingers enrobed in Nestlé milk chocolate, with a sweet cream filling between wafer layers made with palm oil. Per bar: approximately 106 kcal, 5.5g fat (3.5g saturated fat from palm oil and cocoa butter), 13g carbohydrate (9g sugar — equivalent to 36% of the WHO daily added sugar limit of 25g), and ~20mg sodium (negligible). Per 100g: 505 kcal, 26.2g fat, 16.7g saturated fat, 61.9g carbohydrate, 42.9g sugar, 7.1g protein, 1.9g dietary fibre, 95mg sodium. Glycemic index ~55 (low), glycemic load ~7 per bar — the fat content from chocolate and wafer cream moderates glucose absorption below that of plain biscuits. Kit Kat is the most commonly purchased individual confectionery bar in Singapore (Nielsen retail data). The 4-finger bar (42g) delivers 18g sugar (72% of WHO daily limit). For Type 2 Diabetes: not a free food — 9g sugar and GL 7 must be accounted for in the carbohydrate budget; occasional treat (1–2×/week) is appropriate. For cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis: saturated fat content warrants moderation. Halal-certified and vegetarian. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Originally developed by Rowntree's (UK, 1935), now a Nestlé global brand.
common_user_queries: is kit kat ok for diabetes
how much sugar in kit kat

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutritional values sourced directly from Nestlé Singapore product label (2024), which is an approved manufacturer label source for packaged products. Values are internally consistent and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central chocolate wafer bar reference data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate wafer bars, with confidence 0.9.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Nestlé Singapore product label and is not available in the Singapore HPB database for this specific product; USDA FoodData Central does not have an exact match for the Singapore-market Kit Kat formulation. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on product label and not available in approved sources for this specific SKU. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Nestlé Singapore product label (2024). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for chocolate wafer confection per USDA FoodData Central reference data; these are not naturally present in significant quantities in this product type. All core macros (protein_g 7.1, fat_g 26.2, carbohydrate_g 61.9) are non-zero and well-supported. GI ~55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for milk chocolate wafer bars; GL per 21g bar calculated as (55 × 13) / 100 = 7.15, reported as 6.9 consistent with input record.
